summary: Small lbarchive cleanup/fix PR that renamed lbArchive_80017340 to the descriptive lbArchiveRelocate, updated fighter call sites and symbols.txt, removed an apparently redundant non-fatal lbArchive_LoadSections implementation, fixed va_start usage in two varargs archive-loading helpers, and cleaned up a shadowed local declaration in lbArchive_vLoadSectionsFatal.
what_changed: Renamed the global function at .text:0x80017340 from lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Updated the function definition and declaration from lbArchive_80017340 to lbArchiveRelocate in src/melee/lb/lbarchive.c and src/melee/lb/lbarchive.h.
Updated two fighter data call sites in ftData_80085CD8 and ftData_80085E50 to call lbArchiveRelocate when relocating copied x59C archive data.
Removed the standalone lbArchive_LoadSections implementation from lbarchive.c, deleting code that iterated varargs symbol/name pairs and OSReported missing public symbols.
Changed lbArchive_vLoadSectionsFatal to reuse the existing symbol_name local instead of redeclaring a shadowing const char* inside the loop.
Fixed va_start in lbArchive_80017040 and lbArchive_800171CC from va_start(args, filename) to va_start(args, symbols), matching the last named parameter before the varargs.
smart_moves: The rename lbArchiveRelocate captures the actual behavior shown by the function signature and call sites: it accepts an HSD_Archive, source bytes, file size, and base address, then relocates archive contents.
The PR updates the symbol table, header, implementation, and all known call sites together, avoiding a partial rename that would break build or symbol consistency.
The varargs fix uses the correct C convention: va_start must name the final fixed parameter, which is symbols for both affected functions, not filename.
Removing the unused or redundant lbArchive_LoadSections reduced duplicate symbol-loading logic and left the fatal varargs section loader as the remaining implementation in the excerpt.
The shadowed symbol_name cleanup in lbArchive_vLoadSectionsFatal preserves behavior while simplifying the local variable lifetime and avoiding misleading duplicate declarations.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling varargs functions, verify va_start against the function signature rather than against the parameter that conceptually starts the operation; it must use the last named argument before ellipsis.
Descriptive names should be propagated across symbols.txt, headers, implementations, and every call site in one change to keep decomp metadata and C code aligned.
Archive helper names are stronger when they describe the operation, such as Relocate, rather than preserving address-based names after behavior is understood.

Removing duplicate helper implementations can improve maintainability, but the PR context does not include discussion proving whether lbArchive_LoadSections was unused, redundant, or intentionally folded into another helper.
Small cleanup changes like removing a shadowed variable can matter in matching/decomp work because they reduce ambiguity while keeping generated code behavior stable.
naming_conventions: Address placeholder name lbArchive_80017340 was promoted to descriptive name lbArchiveRelocate.
The new name keeps the project’s subsystem prefix style: lbArchive + Operation.
Existing unknown functions lbArchive_80017040 and lbArchive_800171CC remained address-named, indicating only the understood relocation helper was renamed.
The symbol table entry uses the same public symbol spelling as the C function: lbArchiveRelocate.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a semantic rename without changing the function address, size, or symbol scope: .text:0x80017340 remained size 0x17C global.
Kept lbArchiveRelocate signature equivalent to the old function: HSD_Archive*, u8* src, size_t file_size, intptr_t base_addr.
The va_start corrections are source-level bug fixes that may also be necessary for correct generated code around varargs traversal.
The lbArchive_vLoadSectionsFatal local declaration change avoids inner-scope redeclaration while preserving the same va_arg-based symbol lookup flow.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
No human PR body text was included in the provided excerpt, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred from code changes.
follow_up_queries: Check whether lbArchive_LoadSections had external declarations or users before removal.
Audit nearby varargs archive helpers for additional va_start calls that might use the wrong final fixed parameter.
Investigate lbArchive_80017040 and lbArchive_800171CC behavior for possible descriptive names now that their varargs handling is clearer.
Review call sites of lbArchiveRelocate to confirm base_addr calculation patterns for copied archive data.
